settings.title: Wellness Exam
settings.summary: A nose-to-tail check that catches changes in your pet's health early.
settings.description:
Pets age faster than we do, so a yearly once-over makes a real difference. A wellness exam is a chance to catch small changes early, while they are far easier to manage, and to keep a steady record of your companion's health over time.

What we check

We listen to the heart and lungs, check eyes, ears, teeth, and weight, and ask about appetite and behaviour at home. The conversation matters as much as the hands-on part, since you notice the day-to-day shifts we cannot see in the clinic.

  • Heart, lungs, eyes, ears, and teeth
  • Weight, body condition, and coat or skin
  • A chat about appetite, energy, and any changes at home

You leave knowing how your companion is doing and what to keep an eye on. If anything stands out, we will explain it plainly and suggest a sensible next step rather than a long list of worries.

Who benefits most

Every pet gains from a regular check, but older animals and those with ongoing conditions often benefit from coming in more than once a year. We will recommend a rhythm that fits your pet's stage of life.

It helps to jot down anything you have noticed beforehand, even small things like drinking more or slowing on walks. Those details often give us the most useful clues.
